backend/main.mo: Motoko actor implementing per-user radio station favorites storage using a Principal-keyed map, with addFavorite, removeFavorite, and getFavorites endpoints.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting two completed features: a minimalist UI redesign removing decorative HUD elements, and repurposing RadarWidget as a functional playback state indicator.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-line summaries of each frontend source file, describing the purpose and key changes made during the minimalist redesign.
frontend/index.css: Global CSS entry point importing Tailwind layers and defining OKLCH-based light and dark CSS custom properties for the shadcn-ui design token system.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML shell titled 'Military Aircraft Radio' that mounts the React app at the #root div and loads the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that wires together the radio player hook, favorites mutations, station search queries, and renders AppHeader, SearchPanel, NowPlayingPanel, and StationList in a single mobile-viewport layout.
frontend/src/components/AppHeader.tsx: Compact header component rendering the app logo, 'SQUADRON RADIO' title, an inline RadarWidget as a playback state indicator, a search toggle button, and STATIONS/SAVED view tabs.
frontend/src/components/GaugeWidget.tsx: Empty file — the GaugeWidget component has been removed as part of the minimalist UI redesign.
frontend/src/components/HUDLayout.tsx: Thin pass-through wrapper component that applies only a dark background color, with all decorative scanlines, vignette, and grid effects removed.
Compact single-row panel displaying the current station name, status label (ON AIR/TUNING/PAUSED/etc.), and inline playback controls including play/pause/stop buttons and a volume slider.
frontend/src/components/RadarWidget.tsx: A canvas-based animated radar widget that visually reflects radio playback state (playing, loading, paused, error, stopped) by varying sweep speed, blip rendering, and error indicators.
frontend/src/components/SearchPanel.tsx: A search UI panel for filtering radio stations by name, genre tag, and country, with a quick-select tag bar and a filterable country dropdown.
frontend/src/components/StationList.tsx: Renders a scrollable list of radio stations with play state indicators, favicon display, and favorite toggle buttons, supporting both API and backend station types.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A React hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (authenticated or anonymous) using React Query, and invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and hook, managing AuthClient creation, login/logout flow, and identity persistence across page reloads.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching, adding, and removing favorite stations via the ICP backend actor, plus a utility to convert API station objects to backend types.
frontend/src/hooks/useRadioPlayer.ts: A React hook that manages an HTML Audio element for streaming radio, exposing play/pause/resume/stop/volume controls and a playback state enum.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file defining a military HUD-themed design system with OKLCH color tokens, Share Tech Mono/Orbitron fonts, Tailwind layers, custom scrollbar styles, and utility classes for green/amber phosphor aesthetics.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that wraps the App component with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider before mounting to the DOM.
frontend/src/services/radioBrowserApi.ts: Service module for the Radio Browser API providing functions to fetch top stations, search by tag/country/name, list countries, list tags, and register station clicks.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with HUD color tokens (OKLCH-based), Orbitron/Share Tech Mono fonts, custom border radii, box shadows, and keyframe animations for the cockpit UI.
project_state.json: Project state manifest describing the military aircraft-themed radio player app's architecture, existing features, design constraints (no decorative scanlines/radar sweeps), and integration with the Radio Browser API.
